methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Gauss Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Milena Roveda that has raised €9M+ in government funding. The valuation universe comprises 8 private nuclear energy firms and 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR), with notable peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable to Gauss Fusion due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private companies; filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier comparables yields fewer than 3 datapoints, the minimum threshold for statistical reliability. An all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of nuclear subsectors and technology maturity profiles across the peer set.

The analysis therefore cannot support a triangulated valuation at this time. The key uncertainties preventing determination of a defensible range are twofold: the scarcity of Series A-stage nuclear companies with disclosed funding or valuations, and the wide variance in capital intensity and commercialization timelines across the sector. Proceeding would require either (1) expansion of the comp universe beyond nuclear energy into adjacent deep-tech sectors with comparable capex and development risk profiles, or (2) alternative methodologies such as cost-of-capital or discounted-cash-flow frameworks calibrated to government-backed frontier tech deployment timelines.
